mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with rising housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in >58% of cases, or the regime-conditional effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates coinciding with falling total employment create a stagflationary shock, causing immediate negative equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annually signals severe demand weakness, guaranteeing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or the threshold model shows no out-of-sample predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion correlates with negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile labor demand, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases during recovery phases produce positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or the regime-specific signal loses directional accuracy in walk-forward tests.
